user_query:
I'm organizing a Francesc Bonet Fan Club planning meeting and need structured meeting notes plus actionable next steps that reflect his achievements and international recognition. Please do the following using our local materials and fresh web research, and produce the outputs exactly at the paths below:

Inputs you must read:
- input/agenda.md — use this to shape the section headers and overall flow of the meeting notes.
- input/prior_notes.md — extract any items marked [TODO] that are not marked [DONE], [BLOCKED], or [IN REVIEW]; carry them over verbatim.
- input/members.csv — assign owners to action items based on member roles.

Required Internet step (live search, no fixed URLs provided here):
- Perform at least two distinct search engine queries, each containing the phrase "Francesc Bonet" plus at least one of: awards, recognition, biography, interview, festival, press.
- For each query, capture the top 3–5 results you deem most relevant (titles and URLs) and save a machine-readable log to workspace/data/search_log.json with this shape:
  {
    "queries": [
      {
        "id": "Q1",
        "query": "<full search string>",
        "timestamp": "<ISO 8601>",
        "results": [
          {"id": "R1", "title": "<result title>", "url": "<result URL>"},
          {"id": "R2", "title": "...", "url": "..."}
        ]
      },
      {"id": "Q2", ...}
    ]
  }
- Your meeting notes and action items must only cite sources that appear in this log, using inline references in the form [Qn-Rm], where n is the query id number and m is the result id number.

Synthesize and produce these deliverables:
1) workspace/notes/meeting_notes.md — a readable document that:
   - Starts with a title and today’s date.
   - Follows the structure implied by input/agenda.md (use its section headings as your outline) and includes at minimum these sections:
     • Agenda Overview (briefly restate the agenda from input/agenda.md)
     • Highlights of Francesc Bonet’s Achievements & International Recognition (5–8 bullet points paraphrased from your found sources; include one or more inline citations like [Q1-R2] per bullet)
     • Content Ideas (at least 3 ideas tailored for the fan club; cite sources where appropriate)
     • Outreach Plan (at least 2 target organizations or media outlets with a one-line rationale; cite sources)
     • Carried-Over Items from Previous Notes (verbatim list of [TODO] items extracted from input/prior_notes.md)
     • Action Items (a consolidated list of new items you propose plus carried-over items; each item shows an owner name and role from input/members.csv, and if the item is new, include any relevant citation refs)
   - Include a short “Sources” section that explains your citation key format and notes that full titles/URLs are in workspace/data/search_log.json.

2) workspace/notes/action_items.csv — a CSV with columns:
   item,source_section,owner_name,owner_role,status,citation_refs
   - source_section must be one of: Carried-Over, New-Content, Outreach.
   - status is Carry-Over for items taken from input/prior_notes.md; otherwise New.
   - citation_refs contains zero or more [Qn-Rm] tokens separated by semicolons; include citations for new items derived from web sources.
   - Assign owners by matching task type to roles in input/members.csv (e.g., social posts → Social Media Lead; website tasks → Web Admin; research/verification → Research Lead; media/partners → Outreach Coordinator; general coordination/logistics → Chair or Secretary; translation → Translator). If multiple could fit, choose one and be consistent.

Constraints:
- Do not fabricate sources: only cite items present in workspace/data/search_log.json.
- Preserve the original wording of carried-over [TODO] items from input/prior_notes.md in both meeting_notes.md and action_items.csv.
- Make sure every action item in meeting_notes.md appears as a row in action_items.csv with the same wording and the assigned owner.

Deliverables to produce:
- workspace/data/search_log.json
- workspace/notes/meeting_notes.md
- workspace/notes/action_items.csv

The goal is a celebratory, well-cited snapshot of Francesc Bonet’s achievements and international recognition, plus clear, role-assigned next steps for our team. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
